pending.body: Quarantine recovered handoff-finalization errors per repo instead of propagating them out of `process_cycle_multi_repo`. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:428-432`, `phase0_finalize_persisted_cancelled_handoff(...)` is called with `?`, but that helper can return real runtime errors from lease/snapshot/handoff reads (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3802-3844`). In multi-repo mode that means one repo with a corrupt or ambiguous recovered handoff now aborts the entire cycle for every registered repo, instead of isolating the bad repo the way the rest of Phase 0 does.

Block the repo when the new recovered-terminal Phase 0 path hits label-sync or lease-cleanup failure. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:436-464`, failures only mark `label_dirty` and `continue`. But `tasks` was snapshotted before that at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:416-422`, so the later dirty-task quarantine loop (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:485-562`) never sees the just-dirtied task in the same cycle. The daemon can therefore keep processing other pending tasks even though `phase0_release_terminal_task_lease` already failed and the recovered task still has contradictory durable state.
